what: What other votes correlate with the 2014 vote on immigration (mei)?
title: Find and plot correlated vote results
text: sake illustration interested result 2014 vote immigration Swiss municipalities. initiative voted upon 9th February 2014. different ways find exact vote. One using information title. solution look id provided FSO. unique(federalvotes$name[grep("Massen", federalvotes$name)]) First, invoke necessary packages use function get_nationalvotes access data. specify unit analysis well range.
code: # installation from CRAN (stable) # install.packages("swissdd") # install.packages("dplyr")  # installation from github (ongoing updates) # devtools::install_github("politanch/swissdd")  library(swissdd) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(tidyr)  #get results of all votes between 2010-2019 federalvotes <- get_nationalvotes(geolevel = "municipality",                                    from_date = "2010-03-07",                                    to_date = "2020-09-27")  #get correlations for votes on municipal level with mei simvotes <- similar_votes(federalvotes, id=5800, from=.4, to=.6) simvotes #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   id    correlation #>   <chr>       <dbl> #> 1 6350        0.591 #> 2 5990        0.563 #> 3 5710        0.494 #> 4 5960        0.454  #extract names of correlated votes ballotnames <- federalvotes %>%   dplyr::select(name, id, mun_id)%>%   filter(id%in%c(5800, simvotes[2,1]))%>%   distinct(name)   #get correlations for votes on municipal level with mei simvotes <- similar_votes(federalvotes, id=6310, from=.3, to=1) simvotes #> # A tibble: 22 × 2 #>    id    correlation #>    <chr>       <dbl> #>  1 5800        0.911 #>  2 5970        0.906 #>  3 6240        0.898 #>  4 5523        0.877 #>  5 5521        0.875 #>  6 5880        0.786 #>  7 5760        0.774 #>  8 5890        0.762 #>  9 5610        0.713 #> 10 5790        0.707 #> # ℹ 12 more rows  #extract names of correlated votes ballotnames <- federalvotes %>%   dplyr::select(name, id, mun_id)%>%   filter(id%in%c(6310, simvotes[1,1]))%>%   distinct(name)  #subset for correlated votes corrvotes <- federalvotes %>%    filter(id%in%c(6310, simvotes[1,1]))%>%   dplyr::select(id, jaStimmenInProzent, mun_id)%>%   mutate(id=as.character(id))  #plot corrvotes%>%   pivot_wider(names_from="id", values_from="jaStimmenInProzent")%>%   ggplot(aes(y=`6310`, x=`5800`))+   geom_point(alpha=.2)+   scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,100))+   scale_x_continuous(limits=c(0,100))+   geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope=1,  size=.1)+   geom_smooth(method="lm", size=.3, color="orange")+   labs(y="BGI, Yes Shares", x="MEI, Yes Shares", caption="politan.ch")+   theme_bw()

title: Plot vote shares
text: Producing map “hand” allows flexibility. example, simply download geo-information plot something entirely else. never ever want produce plot hand, skip next sectionn. Combine two data.frames. Plot whole thing accordingly.  can start prettifiyng map according liking. Wee add lakes well :
code: # installation from CRAN (stable) # install.packages("swissdd") # install.packages("dplyr")  # installation from github (ongoing updates) # devtools::install_github("politanch/swissdd")   library(swissdd) packageVersion("swissdd") #> [1] '1.1.6' library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(sf) library(viridis)  #download geo information geo_canton <- get_geodata(geolevel = "canton") geo_canton$canton_id <- as.numeric(geo_canton$canton_id)  # download data from API on the vote calles «Swiss coalition for Corporate justice SCCJ» kovi_nat <- get_nationalvotes(votedates="2020-11-29", geolevel = "canton")%>%   dplyr::filter(id == 6360)%>%   dplyr::select(canton_id, jaStimmenInProzent)%>%   mutate(canton_id=as.numeric(canton_id)) can_df <- left_join(geo_canton, kovi_nat, by="canton_id") ggplot(can_df)+   geom_sf(aes(fill=jaStimmenInProzent), color="white", size=.1) lakes <- swissdd::get_geodata(geolevel = "lakes") ggplot(can_df)+   geom_sf(aes(fill=jaStimmenInProzent), color="white", size=.1)+   geom_sf(data=lakes, fill="aliceblue", color=NA)+   scale_fill_viridis(option="D",                       name = "Yes-Share",                      guide=guide_colorbar(title.position="top",                                            direction = "horizontal",                                           barheight = unit(2, units = "mm"),                                           barwidth = unit(50, units = "mm"),                                           title.hjust =0.5))+   theme_void()+   theme(legend.position="bottom")+   labs(title="Result for Vote on Corporate Justice SCCJ", subtitle="November 29, 2020",        caption="data: FSO/{swissdd}")

title: Predict vote shares
text: First, make sure machine connected internet. function get_swissvotes fetches data stored within swissvotes database. Swissvotes offers one comprehensive data platforms relation Swiss referendums initiatives. default, function simply extracts database provided Swissvotes. However, can specify want codebook well (just codebook matter). Additionally, specification save citation. work data Swissvotes, please cite data accordingly. first extract data select variables need make predictions. reason may believe National Council represents people. Therefore, hope see pattern final votes National Council actual yes shares voting day. download prettify data. calculate share yes votes National Council create scatterplot inspect intuition relationship can seen data. default, function extracts database provided Swissvotes. However, can specify want codebook well (just codebook matter). Additionally, specification save citation. work data Swissvotes, please cite data accordingly. First, extract data database select variables need create prediction. reason might assume National Council represents people. case, reflected similar pattern yes shares National Council later actual yes share popular vote.
code: # installation from CRAN (stable) # install.packages("swissdd") # install.packages("dplyr")  # installation from github (ongoing updates) # devtools::install_github("politanch/swissdd")  library(swissdd) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) #extract data from database swissvotesdata <- get_swissvotes(DB=T, savecitation=F, codebook=F) %>%   #select variables we need   dplyr::select(anr, volkja.proz, nrja, nrnein) %>%   #make numeric and rename variables   mutate(nrja = as.numeric(nrja),          nrnein = as.numeric(nrnein),          volkja.proz= volkja.proz/100)%>%   rename(bfsnr=anr,           yesshare = volkja.proz)  #clean data swissvotesdata[swissvotesdata=="."] <- NA  #calculate share of yes votes in national council swissvotesdata <- swissvotesdata %>%   mutate(nr_yesshare = nrja / (nrja+nrnein))

title: Predict vote shares
text: downloading preparing data, calculate share yes votes National Council create scatterplot inspect intuition relationship can seen data.  seems positive correlation (surprise!)… next step, estimate basic model predict vote shares two national votes February 9th, 2020. order , split data training set prediction set. seems positive correlation (surprise!)…
code: plot1 <- ggplot(data=swissvotesdata, aes(x=nr_yesshare, y= yesshare))+   geom_hline(yintercept=.5, linetype="dashed", color="grey70")+   geom_point()+   geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope=1, size=.1)+   scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,1), labels=scales::percent)+   scale_x_continuous(limits=c(0,1), labels=scales::percent)+   geom_smooth(method="lm", se=T, size=.5, color="#FF6B00")+   labs(y="Actual Outcome of Vote", x="Yes Share in National Council", caption="politan.ch")+   theme_bw()  plot1

what: 3. Predict the results of the upcoming votes
title: Predict vote shares
text: last step estimate basic model predict vote shares two national votes February 9th, 2020. order , split data training set prediction set. can now plot predictions two ballots. Note, crucial correctly report uncertainty, hence, include prediction interval well.
code: #split data estimation_data <- swissvotesdata[!swissvotesdata$bfsnr%in%c(6290, 6300),] prediction_data <- swissvotesdata[swissvotesdata$bfsnr%in%c(6290, 6300),]  #estimate a model fit_m1 <- lm(yesshare ~ nr_yesshare, data=estimation_data)  #predict prediction <- predict(fit_m1, newdata = prediction_data, interval="predict") %>% as.data.frame prediction$bfsnr <- c(6290, 6300) ggplot(prediction, aes(x=as.factor(bfsnr), y=fit, ymin=lwr, ymax=upr))+   geom_pointrange(aes(color="#FF6B00"))+   geom_text(aes(label=round(fit*100, 1)), nudge_x = 0.05)+   scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,1), labels=scales::percent)+   scale_x_discrete(labels=c("Housing\nInitiative", "Law against\ndiscrimination"))+   geom_hline(yintercept=.5, linetype="dashed", color="grey70")+   labs(title="Simple Prediction of Vote-Shares", subtitle="February 9th, 2020", y="Predicted Yes-Share", x="", caption="politan.ch")+   theme_bw()+   theme(legend.position="none",         axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),         panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()) #add the points to the previous plot prediction_data$yesshare[prediction_data$bfsnr==6290] <- prediction$fit[1] prediction_data$yesshare[prediction_data$bfsnr==6300] <- prediction$fit[2]  prediction_data$lwr[prediction_data$bfsnr==6290] <- prediction$lwr[1] prediction_data$lwr[prediction_data$bfsnr==6300] <- prediction$lwr[2]  prediction_data$upr[prediction_data$bfsnr==6290] <- prediction$upr[1] prediction_data$upr[prediction_data$bfsnr==6300] <- prediction$upr[2]  plot1 + geom_point(data=prediction_data, aes(y=yesshare, x=nr_yesshare), color="#FF6B00", size=3)+   geom_linerange(data=prediction_data,aes(ymin=lwr, ymax=upr), color="#FF6B00")

title: Retrieve Swissvotes-Data
text: First, make sure machine connected internet. default, function extracts database provided Swissvotes. However, can specify want codebook well (just codebook matter). Additionally, specification save citation. work data Swissvotes, recommend save citation. don’t specify anything function executed following specifications. use parameter codebook=T function download data direct browser automatically codebook. ’re interested codebook (accessed data already, example), set parameter DB FALSE. Since loaded data already, need download . illustration, interested upcoming vote affordable housing (date vote 9th February, 2020). variable titel_kurz_d titel_kurz_f contains title vote (shorter version official title titel_off_d titel_off_f). Please note letter d indicates German title, letter f gives French title. Since don’t know much initiative can read well. First, find position data use hyperlink provided read .
code: # installation from CRAN (stable) # install.packages("swissdd") # install.packages("dplyr")  # installation from github (ongoing updates) # devtools::install_github("politanch/swissdd")  library(swissdd) library(dplyr) #default swissvotesDB <- get_swissvotes(DB=T, savecitation=F, codebook=F) get_swissvotes(DB=F, codebook=T) #get position in dataset pos_ah <- grep("bezahlbare Wohnungen", swissvotesDB$titel_kurz_d)  #extract url that directs you to more information about the vote mei_url <- swissvotesDB$anneepolitique[pos_ah]  #mei_url #"https://anneepolitique.swiss/prozesse/56996-volksinitiative-mehr-bezahlbare-wohnungen" #access url browseURL(mei_url)

what: the swiss direct democracy R package
title: Get Swiss Federal and Cantonal Vote Results from Opendata.swiss
text: swissdd builds upon real time data service federal cantonal votes provided Federal Statistical Office (FSO) via opendata.swiss. brings results popular votes, aggregated geographical level choice, straight R. Available levels national cantons districts municipalities package wraps real time data vote Sundays. soon ballots close (12:00 ), datastream continuosly updated, data municipalities complete final results available. Additionally, allows access archive retrieve harmonized results national votes since 1981. Additionally, allows access archive retrieve harmonized results national votes since 1981, well loading data swissvotes-database, comprehensive database swiss popular votes. Thanks major contribution David Zumbach latest version contains brand new functions retrieve geodata administrative boundaries provided FSO plot vote result maps. webservice FSO documented opendata.swiss (language settings can found bottom page): https://opendata.swiss/de/dataset/echtzeitdaten--abstimmungstag-zu-eidgenoessischen-abstimmungsvorlagen https://opendata.swiss/de/dataset/echtzeitdaten--abstimmungstag-zu-kantonalen-abstimmungsvorlagen geodata administrative boundaries can found : https://opendata.swiss/de/dataset/geodaten-zu-den-eidgenoessischen-abstimmungsvorlagen
code: # installation from github devtools::install_github("politanch/swissdd")  #realtimedata on vote-sundays or the data of the last votations federalvotes <- get_nationalvotes(geolevel = "district")  #retrieve data for many votes from the archive, either by selecting indiviual dates... federalvotes <- get_nationalvotes(votedates=c("2019-02-10","1984-09-23"), geolevel = "district")  #... or defining a range. federalvotes <- get_nationalvotes(from_date="2017-01-01",to_date="2018-01-01", geolevel = "district")  # the results of cantonal votes are also available (2019-)  cantonalvotes <- get_cantonalvotes(votedates="2019-02-10", geolevel = "municipality")

title: Plot Cantonal Votes — plot_cantonalvotes
text: plot_cantonalvotes plots results cantonal votes choropleth map using ggplot2.

title: Plot Cantonal Votes — plot_cantonalvotes
code: plot_cantonalvotes(   votedate = NULL,   vote_id = NULL,   geolevel = "municipality",   measure = "result",   standardize = T,   language = "DE",   theme = "srf" )

title: Plot Cantonal Votes — plot_cantonalvotes
text: votedate date ballot. Default: recent ballot available. vote_id id vote. Default: first id mentioned data set. geolevel geographical level. Options: district", "municipality" "zh_counting_districts". measure measure color administrative units. Options: "result" yes vote share "turnout" voter turnout given vote. standardize TRUE, scale measure ranges 0 100 percent. Recommended comparisons votes. language defines language. Options: "DE" German, "FR" French, "" Italian "RM" Romansh. theme defines basic appearance map. Five options available: "srf" theme inspired plots Swiss Radio Television, "" "E" viridis color scales magma, inferno, plasma, viridis cividis.

title: Plot Cantonal Votes — plot_cantonalvotes
text: ggplot object

title: Plot Cantonal Votes — plot_cantonalvotes
code: # Plot the most recent cantonal vote plot_cantonalvotes()   # Plot a specific cantonal vote plot_cantonalvotes(votedate = "2020-02-09", vote_id = 104945)

title: Plot National Votes — plot_nationalvotes
text: plot_nationalvotes plots results national votes choropleth map using ggplot2.

title: Plot National Votes — plot_nationalvotes
code: plot_nationalvotes(   votedate = NULL,   vote_id = NULL,   geolevel = "municipality",   measure = "result",   standardize = T,   lakes = T,   language = "DE",   theme = "srf" )

title: Plot National Votes — plot_nationalvotes
text: votedate date ballot. Default: recent ballot available. vote_id id vote. Default: first id mentioned data set. geolevel geographical level. Options: "canton", "district", "municipality" "zh_counting_districts". measure measure color administrative units. Options: "result" yes vote share "turnout" voter turnout given vote. standardize TRUE, scale measure ranges 0 100 percent. Recommended comparisons votes. lakes TRUE, largest Swiss lakes shown color map. language defines language. Options: "DE" German, "FR" French, "" Italian "RM" Romansh. theme defines basic appearance map. Five options available: "srf" theme inspired plots Swiss Radio Television, "" "E" viridis color scales magma, inferno, plasma, viridis cividis.

title: Plot National Votes — plot_nationalvotes
text: ggplot object

title: Plot National Votes — plot_nationalvotes
code: # Plot the most recent national vote plot_nationalvotes()  # \donttest{ # Plot a specific national vote at cantonal level plot_nationalvotes(     votedate = "2014-02-09",     vote_id = 5800,     geolevel = "canton"     )  # }

what: Obtain similarities a vote result shares with other votes — similar_votes
title: Obtain similarities a vote result shares with other votes — similar_votes
text: similar_votes allows obtain correlations specified vote votes.

title: Obtain similarities a vote result shares with other votes — similar_votes
code: similar_votes(   federalvotes = NULL,   id = NULL,   corr = TRUE,   from = NULL,   to = NULL )

title: Obtain similarities a vote result shares with other votes — similar_votes
text: federalvotes tibble data.frame returned 'get_swissvotes'. id identification number vote, needs four digits. Vote 626 (Zersiedelungsinitiative) needs 6260. corr set TRUE default. FALSE return variance-covariance matrix. lower limit correlations. upper limit correlations.

title: Obtain similarities a vote result shares with other votes — similar_votes
text: tibble containing results

title: Obtain similarities a vote result shares with other votes — similar_votes
code: # \donttest{    fedvotes <- get_nationalvotes(geolevel = "canton",from_date = "2010-03-07",to_date="2019-02-10")    #Find correlating votes for the 'Zersiedelungsinitiative', 2019-02-10  results <- similar_votes(fedvotes, id=6260)    #Zersiedelungsinitiative, 2019-02-10, filter stronger correlations (>0.5)  results <- similar_votes(fedvotes, id=6260, from = 0.5)  # }

what: Transform an opendata.swiss national results json into a tibble — swiss_json_to_dfr
title: Transform an opendata.swiss national results json into a tibble — swiss_json_to_dfr
text: swiss_json_to_dfr transforms json containing results selected federal votedate tibble.

title: Transform an opendata.swiss national results json into a tibble — swiss_json_to_dfr
code: swiss_json_to_dfr(   votedate = NULL,   geolevel = "municipality",   dataurl = NULL,   index = NULL,   language = "DE",   call_res )

title: Transform an opendata.swiss national results json into a tibble — swiss_json_to_dfr
text: votedate date ballot. Default: recent ballot available. select multiple ballots use 'get_swissvotes'-function. Format = YYYYMMDD geolevel geographical level results loaded. Options: "national", "canton", "district" "municipality". dataurl url dataset opendata.swiss index selection index resource (last published = 1). language defines language vote title. Options: "DE" German, "FR" French, "" Italian "RM" Romansh. call_res result previous call base API. Optional argument.

title: Transform an opendata.swiss national results json into a tibble — swiss_json_to_dfr
text: tibble containing results

title: Transform an opendata.swiss national results json into a tibble — swiss_json_to_dfr

title: swissdd 1.0.2
text: CRAN release: 2020-01-19 Functions renamed (breaking change): retrieve real time archive results data via opendata.swiss, function get_nationalvotes must used. get_swissvotes turned function allows get Swissvotes-Data. New function allows retrieve Swissvotes-Data added (get_swissvotes) option retrieve counting district level data get_nationalvotes()- get_cantonalvotes()-functions added (affects Zurich Winterthur ) functions adapted new tidyr API, package now depends tidyr >= 1.0.0.

title: swissdd 1.0.1
text: CRAN release: 2019-08-30 Added parent level ids district municipality level results (canton / district) New function calculates correlations votes added: similar_votes() Better error handling fallback votedate information provided opendata.swiss corrupt Improved speed data-retrieval. Comparison: system.time(get_swissvotes(to_date="1983-12-04")) user system elapsed 2.492 0.160 47.024 comparison swissdd version 1.0.0 user system elapsed 3.120 0.276 64.693
